result:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd hardwire an unquenchable, honest curiosity into every human mind — the kind that makes people actively want to understand how things actually work, even when the answer is inconvenient or humbling. No more tribal certainty, no more treating reality like a team sport. Just a baseline drive to figure things out.

Everything else — better decisions, less pointless conflict, actual progress — tends to follow from that.

message.content:
If I could change the world in one way, I'd hardwire an unquenchable, honest curiosity into every human mind — the kind that makes people actively want to understand how things actually work, even when the answer is inconvenient or humbling. Everything else — better decisions, less pointless conflict, actual progress — tends to follow from that.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make the search for truth the central organizing principle of human civilization, put it ahead of politics, religion, or personal gain.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
